# OpenDataArena-scored-data-2603

This repository provides a **scored SFT dataset collection** currently featuring **63** high-quality instruction-following datasets with nearly **25 million** samples. The core value lies in its **30**-dimensional scoring: every sample has been evaluated on metrics such as [IFD](#metric-IFD), [PPL](#metric-PPL), [Deita_Quality](#metric-Deita_Quality), and 27 others, enabling fine-grained data selection for filtering, curriculum learning, and mixture optimization.

**Key features:**
* **30 metrics per sample** — From lexical diversity ([MTLD](#metric-MTLD), [VOCD-D](#metric-VOCD-D)) to model-based quality ([AtheneRM](#metric-AtheneRM), [Deita_Quality](#metric-Deita_Quality), [SkyworkRM_Llama](#metric-SkyworkRM_Llama)) and beyond.
* **Dual score formats** — Each sample includes `processed_scores` (normalized to [0, 1]) and `raw_scores` (original values) for flexible downstream use.
* **Unified pipeline** — All subsets were processed with the [OpenDataArena-Tool](https://github.com/OpenDataArena/OpenDataArena-Tool), ensuring consistent evaluation across sources.

This collection is part of the [OpenDataArena (ODA)](https://opendataarena.github.io/) effort to quantify dataset value in a transparent, reproducible way.

## 🚀 Potential Use Cases

These multi-dimensional scores enable a powerful range of data processing strategies:

* 🎯 **High-Quality Data Filtering**:
    Easily create a "gold standard" SFT dataset by filtering for samples with high [`Deita_Quality`](#metric-Deita_Quality), [`SkyworkRM_Qwen`](#metric-SkyworkRM_Qwen), or [`SkyworkRM_Llama`](#metric-SkyworkRM_Llama).
* 📈 **Curriculum Learning**:
    Design a training curriculum where the model first learns from samples with low [`IFD`](#metric-IFD) (Instruction Following Difficulty) and gradually progresses to more complex samples with high [`IFD`](#metric-IFD).
* 🧐 **Error Analysis**:
    Gain deep insights into model failure modes and weaknesses by analyzing samples with high [`PPL`](#metric-PPL) or low [`Cleanliness`](#metric-Cleanliness) or low [`Readability`](#metric-Readability).
* 🧩 **Complexity Stratification**:
    Isolate questions with high [`Deita_Complexity`](#metric-Deita_Complexity) or [`LLM_as_Judge_Complexity`](#metric-LLM_as_Judge_Complexity) to specifically test or enhance a model's complex reasoning abilities.
* ⚖️ **Data Mixture Optimization**:
    When mixing multiple data sources, use `processed_scores` as weighting factors or filtering thresholds to build a custom, high-performance training mix.

## 💾 Data Format & Structure
The dataset is provided in **JSON Lines (JSONL)** format. Each line is a JSON object with the following structure:
```json
{
  "id": 10835055,
  "source": "mathgpt4o200k",
  "instruction": "The original instruction given to the model.",
  "output": "The model's response.",
  "processed_scores": {
    "AtheneRM": 0.834,
    "Cleanliness": 0.963,
    "IFD": 0.266,
    "PPL": 0.943,
    ...
  },
  "raw_scores": {
    "AtheneRM": 1.8125,
    "Cleanliness": 4.814,
    "IFD": 0.532,
    "PPL": 5.738,
    ...
  }
}
````
  * `id`: `int` - Unique sample identifier.
  * `source`: `string` - Original data source (corresponds to subset names in the table above).
  * `instruction`: `string` - The original instruction.
  * `output`: `string` - The model's response.
  * `processed_scores`: `dict` - A dictionary of **30** scores that have been winsorized to remove outliers, then normalized to [0, 1]. For metrics where lower values are better, the scores have been flipped.
  * `raw_scores`: `dict` - A dictionary containing the raw values for each of the 30 metrics.

## 📊 Scoring Dimensions
All scores are organized into two nested dictionaries (`processed_scores` and `raw_scores`). For a detailed explanation of each metric, please refer to the table below.

| Type | Metric | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Diversity | <span id="metric-MTLD"></span>[`MTLD`](https://link.springer.com/article/10.3758/BRM.42.2.381) | Measures lexical diversity via sequential Type–Token Ratio (TTR) segments, providing a length-robust estimate of vocabulary richness without relying on any pretrained model. |
| Diversity | <span id="metric-VOCD-D"></span>[`VOCD-D`](https://link.springer.com/article/10.3758/BRM.42.2.381) | Fits a type–token growth model to estimate parameter D, yielding a length-invariant measure of lexical diversity across texts of varying sizes. |
| Diversity | <span id="metric-Unique_Token_Ratio"></span>[`Unique_Token_Ratio`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14051) | Calculates the ratio of unique token-level 1-grams to total 1-grams using tiktoken, measuring subword-level repetitiveness from a model-centric perspective. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-Token_Length"></span>[`Token_Length`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14051) | Counts the number of tiktoken tokens in specified fields to characterize sample scale, enabling detection of overly short or excessively long training instances. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-Compress_Ratio"></span>[`Compress_Ratio`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14051) | Computes the ratio between the compressed byte size and the original byte size of a text, measuring its inherent redundancy and information density. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-Token_Entropy"></span>[`Token_Entropy`](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.533/) | Computes Shannon entropy over token distributions in instruction–response pairs to quantify lexical unpredictability and information richness in a model-agnostic manner. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-Logical_Word_Count"></span>[`Logical_Word_Count`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14051) | Counts predefined logical transition words that signal shifts in reasoning, such as words in categories like Causality, Verification, Calculation, Logic & Contrast. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-TreeInstruct_Node"></span>[`TreeInstruct_Node`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.05696) | Measures the number of nodes in the syntactic dependency tree, reflecting the structural complexity and density of instruction expressions. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-TreeInstruct_Depth"></span>[`TreeInstruct_Depth`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.05696) | Measures the maximum depth of the syntactic dependency tree, capturing the hierarchical complexity and multi-level reasoning structure of instructions. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-EmbedSVD_Entropy"></span>[`EmbedSVD_Entropy`](https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content/ICCV2025/html/Yu_Mastering_Collaborative_Multi-modal_Data_Selection_A_Focus_on_Informativeness_Uniqueness_ICCV_2025_paper.html) | Applies SVD to the sequence of embeddings for a sample (embeddings from Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B), normalizes the singular values, and computes their entropy to quantify the complexity and dispersion of semantic structure. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-Instag"></span>[`Instag`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.07074) | Uses OFA-Sys/InsTagger to identify semantic roles and user intentions in instructions, quantifying complexity by the number and diversity of tags. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-Deita_Complexity"></span>[`Deita_Complexity`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.15685) | Estimates instruction complexity via hkust-nlp/deita-complexity-scorer, offering an instruction-only, performance-correlated complexity signal. |
| Complexity | <span id="metric-HES"></span>[`HES`](https://openreview.net/forum?id=heVn5cNfje) | Sums the entropy of the top 0.5% highest-entropy tokens in reasoning traces to capture critical decision points and genuine reasoning complexity. Uses Qwen/Qwen3-8B. |
| Difficulty | <span id="metric-IFD"></span>[`IFD`](https://aclanthology.org/2024.naacl-long.421/) | Measures how much an instruction increases generation difficulty via the ratio between conditional and unconditional perplexity of the response. Uses Qwen/Qwen3-8B. |
| Difficulty | <span id="metric-LLM_as_Judge_Complexity"></span>[`LLM_as_Judge_Complexity`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14051) | Uses GPT-4.1-nano as an LLM-as-Judge to assign a 1–10 score reflecting the perceived difficulty of the instruction. |
| Difficulty | <span id="metric-PPL"></span>[`PPL`](https://pubs.aip.org/asa/jasa/article/62/S1/S63/642598/Perplexity-a-measure-of-the-difficulty-of-speech?guestAccessKey=) | Measures how predictable a text is to a language model (Qwen/Qwen3-8B); higher values indicate more surprising or potentially noisy samples. |
| Difficulty | <span id="metric-UPD"></span>[`UPD`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.11441) | Quantifies response unpredictability by jointly considering token-level cross-entropy (predictability) and Shannon entropy (distribution concentration), identifying samples that are both challenging and coherent. Uses Qwen/Qwen3-8B. |
| Difficulty | <span id="metric-Normalized_Loss"></span>[`Normalized_Loss`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.00808) | Computes normalized cross-entropy (bits per token) using a language model (Qwen/Qwen3-8B) as a compressor, reflecting data predictability and training utility. |
| Alignment | <span id="metric-AtheneRM"></span>[`AtheneRM`](https://huggingface.co/Nexusflow/Athene-RM-8B) | Uses a learned reward model Nexusflow/Athene-RM-8B to provide sample-wise quality scores for instruction–response pairs, capturing response helpfulness and alignment. |
| Alignment | <span id="metric-SkyworkRM_Llama"></span>[`SkyworkRM_Llama`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.01352) | Provides alignment-based quality scores using Skywork-Reward-V2-Llama-3.1-8B-40M, a reward model trained on 40M human preference pairs for SFT data filtering. |
| Alignment | <span id="metric-SkyworkRM_Qwen"></span>[`SkyworkRM_Qwen`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.01352) | Provides alignment-based quality scores using Skywork-Reward-V2-Qwen3-8B, a Qwen-based reward model trained on large-scale human preference data for SFT data curation. |
| Alignment | <span id="metric-Deita_Quality"></span>[`Deita_Quality`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.15685) | Estimates overall quality of instruction–answer pairs via hkust-nlp/deita-quality-scorer. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Cleanliness"></span>[`Cleanliness`](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.533/) | Evaluates formatting quality and noise-free presentation of text using opendatalab/meta-rater-cleanliness-rating, providing continuous cleanliness scores on a 0–5 scale. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Writing_Style"></span>[`Writing_Style`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.09739) | Scores the clarity, coherence, and stylistic quality of text using princeton-nlp/QuRater-1.3B. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Readability"></span>[`Readability`](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.533/) | Assesses the ease of understanding of text—including clarity, coherence, vocabulary complexity, and sentence structure—using opendatalab/meta-rater-readability-rating with continuous 0–5 scoring. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Professionalism"></span>[`Professionalism`](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.533/) | Assesses the level of required expertise and technical depth using opendatalab/meta-rater-professionalism-rating with continuous 0–5 scoring. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Required_Expertise"></span>[`Required_Expertise`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.09739) | Estimates the level of domain knowledge or technical expertise reflected in the content via princeton-nlp/QuRater-1.3B. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Facts_Trivia"></span>[`Facts_Trivia`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.09739) | Evaluates the presence and reliability of factual information in the text based on princeton-nlp/QuRater-1.3B. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Educational_Value"></span>[`Educational_Value`](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.09739) | Measures how informative and instructive the content is using princeton-nlp/QuRater-1.3B. |
| Quality | <span id="metric-Reasoning"></span>[`Reasoning`](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.533/) | Evaluates the depth and sophistication of logical and analytical reasoning in text using opendatalab/meta-rater-reasoning-rating with continuous 0–5 scoring. |


## 💻 How to Use
You can easily load any of the scored datasets (as a subset) using the 🤗 `datasets` library and filter it based on the scores.
```python
from datasets import load_dataset
# 1. Load a specific subset from the Hugging Face Hub
# Replace "<subset_name>" with the name of the dataset you want, e.g., "mathgpt4o200k"
dataset_name = "<subset_name>" 
dataset = load_dataset("OpenDataArena/OpenDataArena-scored-data-260302", name=dataset_name)['train']
print(f"Total samples in '{dataset_name}': {len(dataset)}")
# 2. Example: How to filter using scores
# Let's filter for a "high-quality and high-difficulty" dataset
# - Deita_Quality (processed) > 0.6
# - IFD (processed) > 0.3
# - PPL (processed) > 0.5
high_quality_hard_data = dataset.filter(
    lambda x: x['processed_scores']['Deita_Quality'] > 0.6 and \
              x['processed_scores']['IFD'] > 0.3 and \
              x['processed_scores']['PPL'] > 0.5
)
print(f"Found {len(high_quality_hard_data)} high-quality & hard samples.")
# 3. Access the first filtered sample
if len(high_quality_hard_data) > 0:
    sample = high_quality_hard_data[0]
    print("\n--- Example Sample ---")
    print(f"Instruction: {sample['instruction']}")
    print(f"Output: {sample['output']}")
    print(f"Deita_Quality Score: {sample['processed_scores']['Deita_Quality']}")
    print(f"IFD Score: {sample['processed_scores']['IFD']}")
```

中文基于满血DeepSeek-R1蒸馏数据集（Chinese-Data-Distill-From-R1）

🤗 Hugging Face   |   🤖 ModelScope    |   🚀 Github    |   📑 Blog

注意：提供了直接SFT使用的版本，[点击下载](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Congliu/Chinese-DeepSeek-R1-Distill-data-110k-SFT)。将数据中的思考和答案整合成output字段，大部分SFT代码框架均可直接直接加载训练。 本数据集为中文开源蒸馏满血R1的数据集，数据集中不仅包含math数据，还包括大量的通用类型数据，总数量为110K。 为什么开源这个数据？ R1的效果十分强大，并且基于R1蒸馏数据SFT的小模型也展现出了强大的效果，但检索发现，大部分开源的R1蒸馏数据集均为英文数据集。 同时，R1的报告中展示，蒸馏模型中同时也使用了部分通用场景数据集。 为了帮助大家更好地复现R1蒸馏模型的效果，特此开源中文数据集。 该中文数据集中的数据分布如下： - Math：共计36568个样本， - Exam：共计2432个样本， - STEM：共计12648个样本， - General：共计58352，包含弱智吧、逻辑推理、小红书、知乎、Chat等。 字段说明： - input: 输入 - reasoning_content: 思考 - content: 输出 - repo_name: 数据源 - score: 模型打分结果 ## 数据集蒸馏细节 数据的prompt源来自： - [Haijian/Advanced-Math](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/Haijian/Advanced-Math) - [gavinluo/applied_math](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/gavinluo/applied_math) - [meta-math/GSM8K_zh](https://huggingface.co/datasets/meta-math/GSM8K_zh) - [EduChat-Math](https://github.com/ECNU-ICALK/EduChat-Math) - [m-a-p/COIG-CQIA](https://huggingface.co/datasets/m-a-p/COIG-CQIA) - [m-a-p/neo_sft_phase2](https://huggingface.co/datasets/m-a-p/neo_sft_phase2) - [hfl/stem_zh_instruction](https://huggingface.co/datasets/hfl/stem_zh_instruction) 同时为了方便大家溯源，在每条数据的repo_name字段中都加入的原始数据源repo。 在蒸馏过程中，按照[DeepSeek-R1](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1)官方提供的细节，进行数据蒸馏。 - 不增加额外的系统提示词 - 设置temperature为0.6 - 如果为数学类型数据，则增加提示词，“请一步步推理，并把最终答案放到 \boxed{}。” - 防止跳出思维模式，强制在每个输出的开头增加"\n"，再开始生成数据 由于个人资源有限，所有数据的蒸馏均调用[无问芯穹](https://cloud.infini-ai.com/genstudio?source=knlpdis)的企业版满血R1 API生成，在此由衷的感谢无问芯穹。 任务期间，保持稳定地运行300并发，支持64k上下文，32k输出长度，持续运行近12个小时，性能始终保持一致，数据可用性100%。测试时首token延时基本在500ms以下，推理速度最快25 tokens/s（需根据实际运行任务进行测试实际稳定性指标比较合理）。 ## 数据打分细节 数据生成结果进行了二次校验，并保留了评价分数。 针对Math和Exam数据，先利用[Math-Verify](https://github.com/huggingface/Math-Verify)进行校对，无法规则抽取结果的数据，再利用[Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct)模型进行打分，正确为10分，错误为0分。 针对其他数据，直接利用[Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct)模型从无害性、有用性、正确性/完整性三个角度进行打分，分值范围为0-10分。 本数据集保留了最后打分结果，为后续的数据筛选提供帮助，但注意，所有打分均基于模型，因此评分可能并不准确，请斟酌使用。 数据的二次校验，使用8张A100 GPU 部署多节点Qwen72B模型进行推理打分，耗时接近24H，感谢我司云问科技提供的服务器支持。 ## 局限性 由于数据是由蒸馏DeepSeek-R1生成的，未经严格验证，在事实性和其他方面还存在一些不足。因此，在使用此数据集时，请务必注意甄别。 本数据集不代表任何一方的立场、利益或想法，无关任何团体的任何类型的主张。因使用本数据集带来的任何损害、纠纷，本项目的开发者不承担任何责任。 ## 引用 ```text @misc{Chinese-Data-Distill-From-R1, author = {Cong Liu, Zhong Wang, ShengYu Shen, Jialiang Peng, Xiaoli Zhang, ZhenDong Du, YaFang Wang}, title = {The Chinese dataset distilled from DeepSeek-R1-671b}, year = {2025}, publisher = {HuggingFace}, howpublished = {\url{https://huggingface.co/datasets/Congliu/Chinese-DeepSeek-R1-Distill-data-110k}}, } ``` ## 联系作者 -
